name: 3 Bar Farms
products: Apples, beans, broccoli, carrots, corn, cucumbers, eggplant, flowers, herbs/spices, melons, onions, peas, peppers, pumpkins, rhubarb, squash (summer/winter), tomatoes, eggs, potatoes
description: Nicola Valley Farmers' Market location at corner of Voght St and Merritt Ave. Fresh local eggs, farm animals. Natural practices (not yet certified organic). Cash only.

name: Maan Farms
products: Beans, blueberries, carrots, corn, cucumbers, pumpkins, raspberries, strawberries, tomatoes, vegetables, english peas, hothouse peppers, potatoes, zucchini
description: Picnic area, petting zoo, farm animals, birthday parties, school tours, corn maze (late August-October). Integrated pest management. Pick-your-own: Strawberries (mid-June to mid-July); Raspberries (July-mid-August); Blueberries (mid-July-August). Cash, debit, Visa/MC.

name: Sommer Estate Farms
season: Spring through fall roadside stand; open year-round
products: Seasonal fruits and vegetables, fresh eggs, honey
description: Family-operated farm established in late 1960s; focuses on sustainable practices and beef cattle raising. Certified naturally grown for all crops; sustainable multigenerational farm. Local honey from hives, farm animals. Cash only.

name: Willow View Farms
hours: Petting barn: Sept-Oct Mon-Sat 9am-5:30pm, Sun 11am-5:30pm; Nov-Dec Tue-Sat 10am-5pm
products: Apples, raspberries, blackberries, plums, pears, pumpkins, sweet corn
description: Family-friendly operation with petting zoo, pumpkin patch, playground, picnic areas, hayrides, fall festivals. U-pick raspberries and blackberries (July); pumpkins (fall).

name: North Arm Farm
products: Apples, asparagus, apricots, beans, beets, broccoli, carrots, currants, grapes, herbs/spices, nectarines, onions, pears, peas, peaches, plums, pumpkins, raspberries, rhubarb, squash, strawberries, tomatoes, eggs, baked goods, preserves, jams, jellies
description: Family-owned operation since 1995; open farm allowing visitors to explore how crops are grown. 45+ produce varieties (6 potato varieties, 6 carrot varieties, 5 beet varieties), farm animals (chickens, pigs, sheep), pumpkin patch, free admission, yoga classes, artist workshops. Some organic, some IPM, all sustainably grown. U-pick strawberries, blueberries, raspberries, pumpkins.

name: Squamish Farm Stand
products: Apples, apricots, beans, beets, blackberries, blueberries, broad beans, carrots, cherries, corn, cucumbers, grapes, melons, nectarines, onions, berries, pears, peas, peaches, peppers, plums, pumpkins, raspberries, rhubarb, squash, strawberries, tomatoes, vegetables, eggs
description: Located off Highway 99 at Cleveland Avenue intersection in Squamish. Roadside stand, uncovered outdoor market. Cash, cheque.

name: Blind Rooster Berry Farm
products: Raspberries, Haskap, Saskatoons, fresh eggs
description: No pesticides used. Haskap season mid-May to early July; Saskatoon season mid-June to mid-July; raspberries two crops (mid-June to mid-July, mid-August to end of August). Cash, cheque.

name: Kelowna Farmers & Crafters Market
products: Farm fresh produce, fruits, organic items, jams, preserves, home baking, meats, ethnic foods, soaps, body products, flowers, plants
description: Provides local entertainment and concessions.

name: Little Church Organics
products: Certified organic seasonal vegetables and fruits: greens, lettuce, beets, carrots, cherries, cucumbers, garlic, herbs, onions, peppers, strawberries, Swiss chard, tomatoes
description: Six-acre certified organic farm established 2010 using sustainable practices. Located near Mission Creek with historical ties to Father Pandosy. Cash, debit, Visa/MC.

name: Armstrong BC Farmer's Market
products: Locally-grown fruits, vegetables, home-baked goods, honey, jams, preserves, pottery, soaps, bedding plants, woodwork, arts & crafts
description: Wide variety of seasonal, locally grown produce.

name: Bellmann Specialty Produce
products: Seasonal fruits and vegetables; some certified organic, all sustainably grown; raspberries available for pick-your-own July 10-August 10
description: Farm stand with restrooms. Cash only.

name: Bright Angel Farm
products: Garden stand produce; farm animals on property
description: Garden stand on residence (not on farm); includes restrooms and farm animals. Cash only.

name: Davison Orchards Country Village
products: Over 20 apple varieties; beans, peas, tomatoes, apricots, watermelon, peaches, cantaloupe, nectarines, squash, zucchini, jalapeños, eggplant, cucumbers, green peppers
description: Country village with market, gift shop, Nan's Pantry, produce barn, two cafés, petting zoo (critter corral), antique tractor display. School tours available.

name: Lake Country Farmers Market
address: Swalwell Park, 10090 Bottom Wood Lake Road
products: Baked goods, grown or handmade items by vendors
description: Small market in grassy area beside water park.

name: Sorrento Village Farmers Market
address: Sorrento Shoppers Plaza, 1240 Trans Canada Hwy
products: Fresh local and organic produce, baked goods, eggs, flowers, jams, preserves, crafts
description: Local musicians entertain; ample parking; leashed pets welcome.

name: Vernon Farmers Market
address: Kal Tire Place Parking Lot, 3445 43rd Ave
products: Local fresh fruit, vegetables, handicrafts, eggs, fresh baked goods
description: Features live entertainment.

name: Vernon Friday Night Market
address: 4900 27th Street Village Green Mall (north end parking lot)
season: First Friday in May to Friday of Thanksgiving
products: Local fresh fruit, organics, vegetables, handicrafts, eggs, meat, fish, fresh baked goods
description: Friday evening market with local vendors.

name: Deane Farms
products: Corn, cucumbers, eggplant, peppers, tomatoes
description: Crops typically available June-September. Cash, cheque.

name: Gatzke Orchard and Farm Market
products: 57 varieties of tree fruits on 10 acres; apples, apricots, cherries, nectarines, peaches, pears, plums, hascaps, raspberries, blueberries, strawberries, garlic, corn, heirloom tomatoes, peppers, squash, beans, peas, carrots
description: Located in Oyama at Kalamalka/Wood Lakes isthmus. Farm breakfasts from 6am, patio café, washrooms. Bus loading lanes available. Pick-your-own tours offered.

name: Hillside Orchards
products: Cherries (June-July), apricots (early July), nectarines (mid-July-September), peaches (mid-July-September), plums (early August), pears (late August), apples (late August-October), beans, corn, beets, pickling cucumbers, tomatoes, melons, peppers, garlic, homemade frozen yogurt, ice cream, local cider, fruit vinegars
description: Fourth-generation family farm (25 acres) between Oliver & Osoyoos with desert rolling hills and vineyard views. Unique farm market with fresh produce, jams, syrups, honey. Cash, cheque, debit, Visa/MC.

name: Naramata Community Market
products: Fresh local vegetables, fruit, blueberries, baking, organic honey, salad dressings, arts, crafts, local wines
description: Sampling and wine sales available.

name: Osoyoos Market on Main
hours: May-September 9am-1pm; winter indoor market at Sonora Centre (8505 68 Ave)
products: Seasonal fruits, vegetables, baked goods, preserves, jams, jellies, crafts, artistic products
description: All products sold are made local, grown local. Features live music. Winter markets include Seedy Saturday.

name: Peachland Farmers and Crafters Market
products: Seasonal fruits and vegetables
description: Located in Heritage Park on Okanagan Lake shoreline with Rotary Pavilion, children's playground, picnic tables, spacious grass area. Growing vendor base offering diverse products.

name: Penticton Farmers Market
products: Farm fresh vegetables, fruits, eggs, honey, baking, preserves, handmade crafts
description: All grown or produced in the South Okanagan and Similkameen Valley.

name: Blueberry Patch Country Market
season: March to December; U-pick end of May through mid-October
products: Strawberries, blueberries, raspberries, cherries
description: Hydroponic greenhouse with approximately 20,000 everbearing strawberry plants. Pesticide-free berries, homemade blueberry pie, smoothies, country market with gourmet foods and gift items. Located approximately 1km east of the Creston airport on Highway 21.

name: Soda Creek Sweet Corn and Dunlevy Ranch
products: Large vegetable selection, herbs, corn, fresh local meats
description: Operation features acres of corn and offers a wide variety of vegetables annually, plus locally sourced meat products. The farm concludes its season with fireworks. Cash, debit, Visa/MC.

name: Belle Meadow Farm
season: June 15 - September 15 (fresh fruit); preserves and baked goods year-round on request
products: Fresh fruit, frozen fruit, preserves, jams, jellies, baked goods, haskap berries
description: Roadside stand using natural growing practices (not certified organic). Features restrooms and picnic area.

name: Downtown Salmon Arm Farmers Market
description: Farmers market with multiple vendors.

name: Hanna and Hanna Farm Market and Garden Center
products: 40+ apple varieties, seasonal fruits/vegetables, cider, apple juice, honey, crafts
description: Full-service farm market with garden center, indoor/outdoor covered areas, gift shop, restrooms, school tours, design and landscape services.

name: Salmon Arm Twilight Market
description: Evening farmers market in mall parking lot.

name: Shuswap Farm & Craft Market
products: Fresh farm products, homebaked goods, local crafts, woodworking, bedding plants
description: All made, baked or grown locally.

name: Sandy Acres Berry Farm
products: Strawberries (July 1-Sept 30), loganberries (July 20-Aug 15), raspberries (July 20-Aug 15), cherries (July 15-Aug 31), blackberries (Aug 10-Sept 30), blueberries
description: Farm stand/market on farm with indoor seating, restrooms, picnic area, school tours. Cash only.

name: Barriere Farmer's Market
address: Near Sam's Pizza, 4307 Yellowhead Highway 5 South
products: Homemade/homegrown fruits, vegetables, baked goods, free-range eggs, local artwork
description: Friendly indoor market with ample parking for large vehicles, restaurant access, participates in BC Farmers' Market Nutrition Coupon Program.

name: Desert Hills Ranch
products: Seasonal local fruits, vegetables, pumpkins
description: Offers seasonal events including Easter egg hunts, pumpkin cannons, and wagon rides. Gift shop, concession stand, picnic area, pumpkin patch, petting zoo, bouncy castle, farm animals, birthday parties, weddings.

name: Didar Berry Farm
products: Blueberries
description: Farm stand/market on operating farm with porta-potties and picnic area. Look for the large red barn on 104th Street. Cash only.

name: LeeBerry Farms
products: Blueberries
description: Berry farm offering seasonal blueberries. Debit, Visa/MC.

name: Rondriso Farms Pumpkin Patch and General Store
hours: See website for current hours; Pumpkin patch October 1-31
products: Seasonal fruits/vegetables, eggs
description: Uses integrated pest management; offers farm animals, birthday parties, school tours, picnic area. Cash, cheque, debit.

name: Surrey Farms
products: Pumpkins, strawberries, raspberries, blueberries, currants, gooseberries, seasonal vegetables, fresh-baked pies, homemade jams
description: Family farm offering u-pick in supervised fields; BC-produced items in season.

name: GMS Berry
products: Wide variety of seasonal, locally grown fruits and vegetables, blueberries
description: Call ahead to confirm berry availability. Cash only.

name: Jay's Blueberry Farm
products: Fresh picked and frozen blueberries
description: Family-owned business selling no-spray blueberries picked fresh daily by hand. Frozen blueberries available year-round.

name: Kingfisher Farmers Market
hours: Tuesdays 11am-6pm (July-August); Roadside stand available; Friday online orders
products: Organically grown, local, in season vegetables, honey, meat, cheese, eggs, bread and flowers
description: Service area: White Rock/South Surrey.

name: Alouette Tree Farm Ltd
products: Blueberries, gifts, refreshments
description: Garden stand on a residence (not on a farm) offering blueberries, gift shop, concessions/refreshment stand, restrooms, picnic area, petting zoo, farm animals. Cash, cheque, debit, Visa/MC.

name: Sandhu Farms
products: Blueberries
description: Minimizes chemical and pesticide use - focuses on sustainable farming practices. Restrooms on-site. Cash only.

name: Berry Best Farm

description: Environmentally friendly, family run, fruit and vegetable farm. Also sells produce at farmers markets and Edible Island Whole Foods Market.
region: Powell River / Comox Valley - Comox Valley

description: Farm stand/market with indoor and covered outdoor facilities. Minimizes chemical and pesticide use. Specializes in bulk items like 50-pound potato sacks and cabbage. Cash, debit, Visa/MC.

description: Wide variety of seasonal, locally grown fruits and vegetables with garden centre.
